text.title: Social Proof Harvest Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Social Proof Harvest application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Detection MUST be automated (saved search/alert/scraper) running ≥daily; manual scrolling is rejected.
rule.rationale: Solo founders skip manual detection in busy weeks and lose months of signal.

rule.statement: Capture MUST record verbatim quote + author identity + URL + timestamp; paraphrased quotes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Paraphrased quotes attributed to a real person create legal exposure under FTC §255.

rule.statement: Consent MUST be explicit + logged with timestamp before status flips to approved.
rule.rationale: Public visibility on a personal handle is not consent to commercial use; GDPR + personality rights require logged consent.

rule.statement: Every wall entry MUST link back to the original public mention; uncited blurbs are rejected.
rule.rationale: Cited quotes earn 4-6x more trust than uncited ones; broken links erode the same trust.

rule.statement: Quote DB MUST be the single source-of-truth feeding all surfaces (wall, landing, deck, email); duplicating quotes across stores is rejected.
rule.rationale: Diverging copies create stale wording and turn one consent record into many ambiguous claims.
